arts.ttl: Star who played key role in Newcastle’s revival under Eddie Howe now set to be sold
arts.sum: Newcastle United are reportedly willing to sell veteran right-back Kieran Trippier in the upcoming January transfer window, signalling the potential end of his influential spell at St. James’ Park, according to Football Insider. The 34-year-old, whose contract runs until the summer of 2026, has seen his playing time reduced this season. Trippier has started only […] The post Star who played key role in Newcastle’s revival under Eddie Howe now set to be sold appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Mohamed Salah looking to stay at Liverpool
arts.sum: Liverpool star and top scorer Mohamed Salah is looking to extend his stay with the club, however, the board has not made any approach to offer a new contract. Although a few weeks ago the player assured that his departure from Liverpool was closer than a renewal, in various interviews he has made clear his love for the club, the city and his desire to stay.

arts.ttl: Nine arrests at Liverpool v Manchester City after reports of tragedy chanting
arts.sum: Man charged with public order offence at Anfield Kian Holt will appear before magistrates in January Nine men have been arrested following reports of tragedy chanting at the Premier League match between Liverpool and Manchester City on Sunday. Merseyside Police confirmed the nine, from Greater Manchester, Lancashire and Cheshire, were arrested at Anfield. A 28-year-old man from Prestwich was also arrested on suspicion of common assault. Kian Holt, 19, of Medlar-with-Wesham in Lancashire has been charged with a public order offence under the Football Offence Act 1991, namely tragedy chanting. He is due to appear at Sefton Magistrates Court on 8 January. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Man charged after tragedy chanting arrests at Liverpool vs Man City
arts.sum: A 19-year-old man has been charged with tragedy chanting during the Premier League match between Liverpool and Manchester City on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Watch ecstastic boyhood Liverpool fan react as he is drawn against them in the FA Cup third round - as Accrington Stanley star Josh Woods prepares for the game of a lifetime
arts.sum: Woods, 24, is a forward for League Two side Accrington Stanley and is more used to assignments against Swindon Town or Newport County.
